sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Angela, presents with a history of recurrent kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode.
She reports that the pain has been up and down, and she was previously treated in the emergency room last week.
The pain has since resolved, and she denies any current pain or blood in her urine.
She has a past medical history of hypertension and diabetes, for which she is on norvasc 2.5mg daily and metformin 500mg daily.
She reports that her blood pressure has been well-controlled at home, but her last blood pressure reading in the office was 150/90.
She also has a history of insomnia, which she has been managing with meditation apps.
Past medical history: Angela has a history of recurrent kidney stones, hypertension, and diabetes.
She is on norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension and metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.
Review of systems: No other symptoms mentioned.
Current medications: Norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension, metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 150/90, no other vital signs mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's lungs are clear bilaterally, with no crackles heard on auscultation.
The heart exam reveals a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The abdomen exam shows no tenderness to palpation or on the CVA on the right side.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a history of recurrent kidney stones, which appear to have passed this time, but further evaluation is needed to rule out any underlying issues.
The patient's hypertension is well-controlled on norvasc, but further monitoring is needed.
The patient's diabetes is being managed with metformin, but further education on proper diet and glucose monitoring is needed.
Problem list: Kidney stones; Hypertension; Diabetes; Insomnia.
Plan:
I prescribed 10mg of melatonin as needed for insomnia.
I gave the patient a referral to a urologist to evaluate her recurrent kidney stones.
I advised the patient to start taking her blood sugars before every meal to monitor her glucose levels.
I also gave the patient a consult to a nutritionist to help her manage her diet and eventually reduce her medication burden.
Follow-up: The patient will return in three months for a follow-up appointment.
